title: HTTP/2 Frames, Streams, HPACK, Flow Control, and Go Boundaries · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs http/2 frames, streams, hpack, flow control, and go boundaries from bounded byte and state evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the HTTP/2 connection, stream, frame, and compression model. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for HTTP/2 connection, stream, frame, and compression model after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace connection preface, SETTINGS exchange and acknowledgement, frame header, payload length, type, flags, stream identifier, and connection errors.
Apply idle, open, half-closed, closed, reserved, reset, and connection shutdown transitions independently per stream.
Map pseudo-fields and regular fields, enforce ordering and connection-field rules, bound HPACK tables and header lists, and avoid indexing secrets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review H2 Go Transfer Boundary
steps.instruction:
review case — A review protocol team handles case 3326 in a field-normalizing cache with an independent origin parser. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine HTTP/2 frame, stream, flow-control, and header-list bounds; preserve this operating constraint: the maintained Go protocol stack remains the production transfer target; then inject Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length and close before any downstream forwarding. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use Go 1.26 net/http and current HTTP/2 implementation for production while limiting custom work to models, frame labs, tests, and authorized disposable environments.

Inspect the H2 Go Transfer Boundary trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review H2 Go Transfer Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use go 1.26 net/http and current http/2 implementation for production while limiting custom work to models, frame labs, tests, and authorized disposable environments. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review H2 Go Transfer Boundary, keep H2 Go Transfer Boundary connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use Go 1.26 net/http and current HTTP/2 implementation for production while limiting custom work to models, frame labs, tests, and authorized disposable environments.

hints: For Record what required effort · Review H2 Go Transfer Boundary, name the types, ownership, and stop path, H2 Go Transfer Boundary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Record what required effort · Review H2 Go Transfer Boundary, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review protocol team handles case 3333 in a canary edge service advertising HTTP/3 with bounded fallback. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine HTTP/2 frame, stream, flow-control, and header-list bounds; preserve this operating constraint: one parser state transition may consume bytes only after its full precondition holds; then truncate one content octet and classify incomplete framing without connection reuse. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use Go 1.26 net/http and current HTTP/2 implementation for production while limiting custom work to models, frame labs, tests, and authorized disposable environments.

For Record what required effort · Review H2 Go Transfer Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A training frame decoder should replace the maintained Go HTTP/2 stack in production.
